visualGoal: Make the six-axis-across-two-sticks layout instantly legible so a new HOSAS pilot stops fearing the controls menu. This is a low-product-density how-to: lean on the axis-map infographic and the twist decision tree, not on product cards. Only genuinely relevant sticks appear as real product proof (the hardware you actually bind).

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Referenced text-only for its lockable twist axis (the cleanest hardware answer to the disable-twist-on-the-aiming-stick step). Has a data record but no verified in-house image. Source a real photo before it can appear as a gearpick/shotgrid or before certification if it should show visually.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: First-person cockpit view of a HOSAS desk setup at a home battlestation, two flight sticks mounted at ergonomic angles with open product-overlay space on the left and right grips for real stick photos, subtle magenta/cyan nebula glow through an implied canopy, clean editorial 16:9, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI text.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake sticks, warped grips, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: The standard HOSAS axis map: right rotates, left translates
content: RIGHT STICK (aiming) = rotation: X tilt Roll, Y tilt Pitch, Twist Yaw (or disabled)
LEFT STICK (maneuvering) = translation: X tilt Lateral strafe, Y tilt Forward/back thrust, Twist Vertical strafe
Thumb slider/ministick: zoom or throttle (right), cruise (left)
Callout: twist on the aiming stick is the one decision
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, two-stick split layout, large readable labels, arrows from each physical input to its ship axis, space colors; real stick photos as the left/right column headers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Which twist fix should you use?
content: Stick-only -> disable right twist, yaw on left X tilt, strafe to hat/left twist
Relative mouse aim -> leave yaw on right twist, lateral strafe on left X tilt
Grip has lockable twist (Virpil ALPHA) -> lock right hand twist in hardware, keep left live
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered/branching decision tree, IgnitionSim styling, three clear outcome cards, large text, space accent colors

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult space-sim curator Val Chen at a dual-stick HOSAS battlestation, one hand resting on each flight stick, calm methodical energy, glowing controls menu implied on an off-screen monitor, product overlay space beside the sticks, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.
